title: Brain mechanisms in religion and spirituality: An integrative predictive processing framework.

assets.abstract: We present the theory of predictive processing as a unifying framework to account for the neurocognitive basis of religion and spirituality. Our model is substantiated by discussing four different brain mechanisms that play a key role in religion and spirituality: temporal brain areas are associated with religious visions and ecstatic experiences; multisensory brain areas and the default mode network are involved in self-transcendent experiences; the Theory of Mind-network is associated with pra

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Visionary cosmologies — self-transcendence, mystical experience, numinous, awe, cosmic consciousness.
